summary: A high‑energy 1970 out‑take with a monster Other One and Viola Lee Blues; soundboard source that’s generally good despite some tuning quirks.
review: The performance is praised for its soaring Other One, fiery Viola Lee Blues, and an unforgettable run through St Stephen, Cosmic Charley, The Dew, Man’s World, Dancin’ and Cold Rain & Snow. While the soundboard recording benefits from EQ tweaks, listeners note occasional out‑of‑tune moments (especially on Cold Rain & Snow) and a mono electric set, but overall the show is considered a must‑listen classic.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4–5 stars and highlight many standout songs; technical complaints are limited to tuning quirks and mono mix, so the show’s musical strength outweighs recording imperfections.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear soundboard source but benefits from EQ; some reviewers note a mono electric set, occasional out‑of‑tune sections (Cold Rain & Snow) and advise avoiding SRS WOW distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Listeners love the overall flow, though several note missing songs from the original DP 8 set and a mono acoustic portion; despite these gaps the performance feels cohesive and transcendent.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Out‑of‑tune issues noted (Gibson SG neck problem), but his recovery and overall tone are praised.
